questions.question: What is realistic SDR quota in Month 1?

options.text: 50% of full quota (4-5 meetings if full is 9)

questions.explanation: Month 1 realistic quota is 50%. Weeks 1-2 are onboarding (zero meetings expected). Week 3: first outreach goes live, maybe 1 meeting. Week 4: 2-3 meetings. Total: roughly 50% of full quota.

questions.question: What are the leading indicators of success visible in Week 2?

options.text: Activity quality, ICP targeting accuracy, and message personalization

questions.explanation: Leading indicators (activity quality, ICP targeting, personalization) are visible in Week 2. Lagging indicators (meetings booked, quota) don't tell you anything useful until Month 3. Manage leading indicators early.

questions.question: According to Bridge Group research, how long is SDR ramp time to full productivity with structured onboarding?
questions.explanation: SDR ramp time is 3-6 months to full productivity without structured onboarding, but structured onboarding cuts it to 2-3 months. The 30-day onboarding playbook from Lesson 6 accelerates this significantly.

questions.question: If your SDR hasn't booked any meetings by the end of Week 2, you should be concerned.
questions.explanation: False. Weeks 1-2 are onboarding — zero meetings is expected. The hire should be listening to calls, learning the ICP, and preparing their first sequences. Look at leading indicators (quality of questions they ask, ICP understanding) instead.

questions.question: Define your Month 1 expectations for a new sales hire. What leading indicators would you track in Weeks 1-2? What lagging indicators would you evaluate at Month 3? What specific 'green lights' would give you confidence?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response sets realistic Month 1 quota expectations (50%), identifies specific leading indicators for early weeks (ICP knowledge, personalization quality, reply rates), defines Month 3 evaluation criteria, and lists concrete green lights from the lesson.
